channel.title: Sandwiches on Gourmand Syndrome
channel.description: Recent content in Sandwiches on Gourmand Syndrome

item.title: Shorty's
item.description: Summary This place is near Stone Street down in the Financial District. I'm always down for a good cheesesteak, and yeah I also butcher the Philly style by ordering provolone over cheeze whiz. The steak special comes with homemade broccoli rabe, provolone cheese, and I also added hot cherry peppers. These are the cherry peppers that are pickled with a nice kick to it. I thought this sandwich was pretty good, though I still prefer Federoff's in Williamsburg.

item.title: Pastrami Plaza
item.description: Pastrami was ok. It was moist yet tips were dry. Food was cheap though, so might be good for lazy dinners. They have other things.

item.title: Tal Bagels
item.description: Believe it or not, their pastrami was actually better than Pastrami Queen.

item.title: Roccos Pizza Joint
item.description: Got the cheesesteak, and I regret it. This place is eh.

item.title: Hank's Juicy Beef
item.description: The only standalone Italian beef sandwich in the city. The other is at the Billsburg Smorgasburg and is a mess to eat. This place is pretty good for fast food.

item.title: Pastrami Queen
item.description: Pretty good pastrami. It's no Katz, and it's not filling, but it was good. It's thin sliced meats.
